definition: The total distance around the outside edge of a shape.
The amount of flat space inside a shape.
The top number in a fraction — it shows how many parts you have.
The bottom number in a fraction — it shows how many equal parts the whole is split into.
A corner point where two or more lines or edges meet.
The longest side of a right-angled triangle, opposite the right angle.
The number placed in front of a variable, showing how many of that variable you have (e.g. the 3 in 3x).
A letter (like x or y) that stands in for a number we don't know yet.
A whole number that divides exactly into another number with no remainder.
A number you get by multiplying a given number by a whole number (e.g. 6, 12, 18 are multiples of 6).
A whole number greater than 1 that can only be divided evenly by 1 and itself.
A way of comparing two quantities, showing how many times one value contains another.
A number expressed as a fraction of 100.
The average of a set of numbers — add them all up and divide by how many there are.
The middle value in a list of numbers when they are arranged in order.
The value that appears most often in a set of numbers.
The difference between the largest and smallest values in a set of numbers.
A measure of how likely something is to happen, usually shown as a fraction, decimal, or percentage.
One of the four sections formed when a graph is divided by its horizontal and vertical axes.
Having exactly the same size and shape.
Having the same shape but not necessarily the same size — one is an enlarged or shrunk copy of the other.
A direction measured clockwise from north, given as a 3-digit angle (e.g. 045°).
A small number showing how many times to multiply a number by itself (e.g. the 3 in 2³).
A square root (or other root) that cannot be simplified into a whole number.
An expression made of variables and numbers combined using addition, subtraction, and multiplication (e.g. 2x² + 3x - 1).
The amount left over when one number doesn't divide exactly into another.
A whole number that can be positive, negative, or zero — never a fraction or decimal.
The distance all the way around a circle.
The distance from the center of a circle to its edge.
The distance across a circle through its center — exactly twice the radius.
A straight line that touches a curve or circle at exactly one point without crossing it.
A list of numbers arranged in a specific order, following a pattern or rule.
A rectangular grid of numbers arranged in rows and columns.
A single number calculated from a matrix that tells you certain properties about it.
A quantity that has both size (magnitude) and direction.
The size or length of a vector, ignoring its direction.
A measure of how steep a line is — how much it goes up or down for every step across.
A value calculated from a quadratic equation that tells you how many solutions it has.
